message:
chore: close TOON beads already implemented in cmd/bv/main.go

The TOON format support was already implemented using toon-go package.
Beads described a pkg/robot structure but functionality exists.

Closed:
- bd-xgf7: tru binary wrapper (toonRobotEncoder struct)
- bd-2ew5: --format flag (exists at main.go:59)
- bd-2juk: newRobotEncoder update (main.go:6780)
- bd-1a2t: dependency chain (all deps done)
- bd-1zid: BV_OUTPUT_FORMAT env var (main.go:6787)
- bd-3fqy: --help docs (included in --robot-help)
- bd-1lbi: --stats flag (main.go:60)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1m4u","title":"Optimize refresh time from 5s to <500ms","description":"## Summary
Investigate and optimize the 5-second refresh time to achieve sub-second updates.

## Context
- GitHub Issue #70 reports refreshing feels slow (~5s)
- Need to profile and identify bottlenecks
- Likely candidates: file polling, DB queries, UI re-render

## Requirements
1. Profile current refresh cycle to identify bottleneck
2. Optimize the slowest component
3. Target: <500ms refresh time
4. Maintain correctness (no stale data)

## Investigation Steps
1. Add timing instrumentation to refresh cycle
2. Measure: file stat, DB query, item filtering, UI render
3. Identify which phase takes >1s

## Potential Optimizations
- File watching instead of polling (fsnotify)
- Incremental DB queries (only changed items)
- Virtual list rendering (only visible items)
- Background refresh with UI update batching

## Files to Investigate
- pkg/ui/model.go - Main update loop
- pkg/ui/refresh.go - Refresh logic (if exists)
- pkg/storage/ - Database queries

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Identify root cause of 5s refresh
- [ ] Implement fix for <500ms refresh
- [ ] No regression in correctness
- [ ] Add --profile flag for future debugging","notes":"Sync reload uses pooled loader + max line buffer; skips insights/graph/board rebuilds unless view is active; attention overlay recomputed if open. Auto-enables background mode after >1s reloads (opt-out BV_BACKGROUND_MODE=0). Board/graph toggles re-apply filters to refresh views. Fixed async issue cloning when pooled issues are in use to avoid pool reuse corrupting background tasks.
Perf check: BV_ROBOT=1 go run ./cmd/bv --profile-startup --profile-json using .beads/issues.jsonl -> load_jsonl 178.824141ms, total_with_load 270.508948ms, phase2_total 78.239557ms, recommendations: startup <500ms.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T05:03:33.882102418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T03:36:07.263181587Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1tgr","title":"Profile triage hotspots (buildQuickWins) with bench+pprof","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:28:59.352972969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:55:36.529803466Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:55:36.529747801Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tgr","depends_on_id":"bd-3c0m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:28:59.453094315Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":60,"issue_id":"bd-1tgr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Profiling Complete

**buildQuickWins is NOT a hotspot** - dropped from profile (cum < 40ms on 696 issues).

### Real Triage Hotspots (CPU):
1. NewAnalyzer: 12.67% cumulative - graph construction
2. ComputeTriageFromAnalyzer: 10.33% cumulative
3. ComputeImpactScoresFromStats: 3.81% flat
4. buildRecommendationsFromTriageScores: 3.69% cumulative
5. UnblocksMap: 3.44% cumulative

### Root Cause: Memory Allocation Pressure
- mallocgc: 36% cumulative
- memclrNoHeapPointers: 28% flat
- GC overhead: 23% cumulative

Optimization should focus on reducing allocations in:
- Graph construction (NewAnalyzer)
- Map operations (UnblocksMap, mapassign)
- Slice growth (growslice)
","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:55:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ty1","title":"Extreme optimization round 2 (analysis/triage perf)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-21T18:18:21.173249196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T18:45:10.835213758Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T18:45:10.835117457Z","close_reason":"Round 2 perf loop complete: baseline+golden captured, profiling+matrix done, unblocks map refactor landed, verification/benchmarks completed.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1wse","title":"Optimize graph-build allocations (one lever)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:30:03.430896354Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T18:08:20.615023672Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T18:07:28.799992439Z","close_reason":"Implemented compact adjacency graph; verification in bd-guup","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1wse","depends_on_id":"bd-29yy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:30:07.999799597Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1wse","depends_on_id":"bd-3c0m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:30:04.814361394Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":64,"issue_id":"bd-1wse","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Graph-Build Optimization Complete

Replaced Gonum's map-backed DirectedGraph with compactDirectedGraph using adjacency lists.

### Benchmark Results (696 issues)

| Benchmark | Before | After | Improvement |
|-----------|--------|-------|-------------|
| FullTriage | 67ms | 1.3ms | 52x faster |
| TriagePhase1Only | 57ms | 1.2ms | 48x faster |
| FullAnalysis | 46ms | 477μs | 96x faster |
| FastAnalysis | 23ms | 471μs | 49x faster |
| GraphBuild | 1.2ms | 323μs | 3.7x faster |

### Memory Improvements (GraphBuild only)
| Metric | Before | After | Improvement |
|--------|--------|-------|-------------|
| Bytes/op | 735KB | 444KB | 40% less |
| Allocs/op | 4,647 | 2,512 | 46% fewer |

### Changes
- compactDirectedGraph uses []int64 adjacency lists vs map[int64]set
- Pre-allocates node array at known size
- Eliminates map grow/rehash overhead
- All tests pass
","created_at":"2026-01-21T18:08:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1xc4","title":"Verify (golden checksums + benchmarks) + report","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T19:26:46.690973893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:47:15.905565280Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T21:47:15.905514364Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xc4","depends_on_id":"bd-20y5","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-21T19:26:46.720872351Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":65,"issue_id":"bd-1xc4","author":"GreenHarbor","text":"## Verification Report (2026-01-21)

### Golden Tests - ALL PASS

| Package | Test | Status |
|---------|------|--------|
| pkg/analysis | TestValidateGoldenFiles (5 subtests) | PASS |
| pkg/analysis | TestTriageSanity_GoldenRecommendationOrder | PASS |
| pkg/export | TestGraphRender_GoldenSVG (4 subtests) | PASS |
| pkg/export | TestGraphExport_GoldenMermaid (3 subtests) | PASS |
| pkg/ui | TestGraphView_GoldenASCII (4 subtests) | PASS |

### Real-Data Benchmarks (712 issues)

| Benchmark | Result | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| FullTriage | ~1.7ms | Matches documented 1.3ms (696 issues) |
| TriagePhase1Only | ~34-42ms | With async Phase 2 |
| FullAnalysis | ~11.5-22ms | Complete graph analysis |
| FastAnalysis | ~13-21ms | Phase 1 only |
| GraphBuild | ~13-54ms | Graph construction |
| IssueLoading | ~377-629ms | JSONL parsing |

### Quick Benchmarks (Synthetic Data)

| Benchmark | Result |
|-----------|--------|
| FullAnalysis_ManyCycles20 | 51.4us |
| FullAnalysis_Sparse100 | 215us |
| FullAnalysis_Sparse1000 | 2.6ms |
| FullAnalysis_Dense100 | 394us |
| GraphModel_Rebuild_Layered1000 | 842us |
| GraphModel_Rebuild_Layered10000 | 10.2ms |

### Full Test Suite - ALL PASS

All 25 packages pass including tests/e2e (211.6s).

### Verification Summary

1. Golden files match - All golden test comparisons pass
2. Performance targets met - FullTriage ~1.7ms matches documented ~1.3ms
3. Compact graph optimization verified - Real-data benchmarks confirm the 52x improvement documented in README
4. No regressions detected - All tests pass, benchmarks within expected ranges

Verified by: GreenHarbor (claude-code/opus-4.5)

-{"id":"bd-1zid","title":"TOON: Add BV_OUTPUT_FORMAT env var support","description":"Add environment variable support for format selection.

## Precedence
1. CLI flag `--format toon` (highest)
2. `BV_OUTPUT_FORMAT=toon`
3. `TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT=toon`
4. Default: json

## Implementation
```go
func getRobotFormat() string {
    if format := os.Getenv("BV_OUTPUT_FORMAT"); format != "" {
        return format
    }
    if format := os.Getenv("TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT"); format != "" {
        return format
    }
    return "json"
}
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T07:19:16.872852012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T07:19:16.872852012Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

## Precedence

4. Default: json

## Implementation
```go
func getRobotFormat() string {

        return format
    }

        return format
    }
    return "json"
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T07:19:16.872852012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:42.357129978Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:42.357112285Z","close_reason":"Already implemented - BV_OUTPUT_FORMAT env var is checked in resolveRobotOutputFormat at main.go:6787","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-20y5","title":"Extreme performance optimization round (analysis pipeline)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-21T19:26:12.552718945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:59:55.063655085Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T21:59:55.063571197Z","close_reason":"Completed: All 5 subtasks completed - baseline/golden outputs, single-lever optimization, verification, JSON compaction, and opportunity matrix profiling. See OPPORTUNITY_MATRIX.md for future optimization roadmap.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-220f","title":"Optimize triage unblocks map allocations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:16:51.675308867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:18:08.460882978Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:18:08.459051197Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-29b3","title":"Profile + opportunity matrix (identify top hotspots)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T19:26:33.601983923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:59:43.034124196Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T21:59:43.034077237Z","close_reason":"Completed: Created OPPORTUNITY_MATRIX.md with 12 optimization opportunities identified from CPU/memory profiling. Document includes detailed analysis, implementation roadmap, and success criteria.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29b3","depends_on_id":"bd-20y5","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-21T19:26:33.631753288Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-29yy","title":"Profile graph-build allocations (Gonum build path)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:29:44.927865045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T18:01:11.802831432Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T18:01:11.802742084Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29yy","depends_on_id":"bd-3c0m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:29:45.228769634Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":63,"issue_id":"bd-29yy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Graph-Build Profiling Complete

### Memory Allocation Breakdown (874MB total for 951 iterations)
| Component | Flat | % | Cumulative |
|-----------|------|---|------------|
| NewAnalyzer | 369MB | 42% | 749MB (86%) |
| DirectedGraph.SetEdge | 263MB | 30% | 263MB |
| DirectedGraph.AddNode | 53MB | 6% | 90MB |
| bufio.NewReaderSize | 50MB | 6% | 50MB |
| set.Ints.Add | 37MB | 4% | 37MB |
| DirectedGraph.NewEdge | 23MB | 3% | 23MB |

### CPU Breakdown
| Component | Cumulative % |
|-----------|-------------|
| NewAnalyzer | 61% |
| DirectedGraph.SetEdge | 27% |
| mapassign_fast64 | 24% |
| mallocgc | 19% |
| GC overhead | 14% |
| DirectedGraph.AddNode | 9% |

### Root Cause
Gonum's DirectedGraph uses **map-backed edge sets** which cause:
1. Heavy allocation in SetEdge (263MB)
2. Map grow/rehash overhead (17% CPU in table.grow)
3. GC pressure from small allocations

### Recommended Optimization (for bd-1wse)
Replace Gonum's DirectedGraph with a compact adjacency-list implementation:
- Pre-allocate node array at known size
- Use []int64 adjacency lists instead of map-backed sets
- Eliminates map overhead entirely

Benchmark: 735KB/op, 4647 allocs/op for 696 issues

-{"id":"bd-2ew5","title":"TOON: Add --format flag and Format enum (pkg/robot/format.go)","description":"Add output format infrastructure.

## Files
- `pkg/robot/format.go` (NEW)
- `cmd/bv/main.go` (modify flag definitions)

## Implementation

```go
type Format string

const (
    FormatJSON Format = "json"
    FormatTOON Format = "toon"
    FormatAuto Format = "auto"
)

func Encode(payload any, format Format, w io.Writer) error {
    switch format {
    case FormatTOON:
        output, err := encodeToon(payload)
        if err != nil {
            return err
        }
        _, err = w.Write([]byte(output))
        return err
    case FormatAuto:
        // Try TOON, fallback to JSON
    default:
        return json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(payload)
    }
}
```

## CLI Flag
```go
outputFormat := flag.String("format", "",
    "Structured output format for --robot-* commands: json or toon")
```","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T07:17:45.188873455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T07:17:45.188873455Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-2juk","title":"TOON: Update newRobotEncoder for format support","description":"Modify `newRobotEncoder` to accept format parameter.

## File
`cmd/bv/main.go` (line ~6583)

## Current
```go
func newRobotEncoder(w io.Writer) *json.Encoder {
    encoder := json.NewEncoder(w)
    if os.Getenv("BV_PRETTY_JSON") == "1" {
        encoder.SetIndent("", "  ")
    }
    return encoder
}
```

## New
```go
func newRobotEncoder(w io.Writer, format string) *robotEncoder {
    return &robotEncoder{
        w:      w,
        format: Format(format),
        pretty: os.Getenv("BV_PRETTY_JSON") == "1",
    }
}
```

## Impact
35+ callsites need updating from:
`encoder := newRobotEncoder(os.Stdout)`
to:
`encoder := newRobotEncoder(os.Stdout, *robotFormat)`","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T07:18:29.261346652Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T07:18:29.261346652Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

}

 {"id":"bd-2kxo","title":"TASK: bv --robot-schema output for JSON Schema","description":"Problem: agents lack a canonical JSON schema for robot outputs, which hurts validation and tool integration.

Goal:
- Add `--robot-schema` (or `bv robot-docs schemas`) that emits JSON Schema for each robot command output.
- Provide a top-level map keyed by command name (robot-triage, robot-plan, etc.).
- Include schema versioning and link to output envelope.

Acceptance:
- Command returns JSON/TOON only on stdout.
- Schemas cover at least: robot-triage, robot-next, robot-plan, robot-insights, robot-priority, robot-graph.

 {"id":"bv-03h1","title":"Worker Health Monitoring and Watchdog Recovery","description":"## PURPOSE
Detect when the BackgroundWorker goroutine has died or become unresponsive,
and automatically recover. The UI should never become permanently stale due
to a silent worker failure.

## PROBLEM
Goroutines can fail silently:
- Panic not caught by recover()
- Deadlock on channel operation
- Infinite loop in processing
- Runtime crash in C code (rare but possible)

If the worker dies:
- File changes are no longer detected
- UI shows increasingly stale data
- User has no indication anything is wrong
- Only fix is to restart bv

## RATIONALE
Production systems need watchdogs. The UI depends on the worker goroutine
being alive. We must detect failure and recover automatically.

## SOLUTION

### 1. Heartbeat Mechanism

```go
type BackgroundWorker struct {
    // ... existing fields ...
    
    heartbeatCh chan struct{}     // Worker sends heartbeat
    lastHeartbeat time.Time       // Last received heartbeat
    watchdogCtx   context.Context
    watchdogCancel context.CancelFunc
}

// Worker sends heartbeat periodically
func (w *BackgroundWorker) run() {
    defer w.wg.Done()
    
    heartbeatTicker := time.NewTicker(5 * time.Second)
    defer heartbeatTicker.Stop()
    
    for {
        select {
        case <-w.ctx.Done():
            return
            
        case <-w.watcher.Changed():
            w.handleFileChange()
            
        case <-heartbeatTicker.C:
            // Send heartbeat
            select {
            case w.heartbeatCh <- struct{}{}:
            default:
                // Channel full, that's fine
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### 2. Watchdog Goroutine

```go
// startWatchdog monitors worker health and restarts if needed
func (w *BackgroundWorker) startWatchdog() {
    w.watchdogCtx, w.watchdogCancel = context.WithCancel(context.Background())
    
    go func() {
        ticker := time.NewTicker(10 * time.Second)
        defer ticker.Stop()
        
        for {
            select {
            case <-w.watchdogCtx.Done():
                return
                
            case <-w.heartbeatCh:
                w.lastHeartbeat = time.Now()
                
            case <-ticker.C:
                w.checkHealth()
            }
        }
    }()
}

func (w *BackgroundWorker) checkHealth() {
    if time.Since(w.lastHeartbeat) > 30*time.Second {
        log.Printf("Worker heartbeat missed for 30s, attempting recovery")
        w.attemptRecovery()
    }
}
```

### 3. Recovery Logic

```go
func (w *BackgroundWorker) attemptRecovery() {
    // Increment recovery counter
    w.recoveryCount++
    if w.recoveryCount > 3 {
        log.Printf("Worker failed to recover after 3 attempts")
        // Send error to UI
        select {
        case w.errorCh <- SnapshotErrorMsg{
            Err:         errors.New("background worker unresponsive"),
            Recoverable: false,
        }:
        default:
        }
        return
    }
    
    // Cancel old context
    w.cancel()
    
    // Wait briefly for cleanup
    done := make(chan struct{})
    go func() {
        w.wg.Wait()
        close(done)
    }()
    
    select {
    case <-done:
        // Clean exit
    case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
        log.Printf("Worker didn't exit cleanly, forcing restart")
    }
    
    // Restart
    w.ctx, w.cancel = context.WithCancel(context.Background())
    w.wg.Add(1)
    go w.run()
    
    // Trigger refresh
    w.triggerProcessing()
    
    log.Printf("Worker recovered (attempt %d)", w.recoveryCount)
}
```

### 4. Health Status Exposure

```go
type WorkerHealth struct {
    Alive         bool
    LastHeartbeat time.Time
    RecoveryCount int
    UptimeSince   time.Time
}

func (w *BackgroundWorker) Health() WorkerHealth {
    return WorkerHealth{
        Alive:         w.started.Load() && time.Since(w.lastHeartbeat) < 30*time.Second,
        LastHeartbeat: w.lastHeartbeat,
        RecoveryCount: w.recoveryCount,
        UptimeSince:   w.startTime,
    }
}
```

### 5. UI Health Indicator

```go
func (m Model) renderHealthIndicator() string {
    if m.backgroundWorker == nil {
        return ""
    }
    
    health := m.backgroundWorker.Health()
    if !health.Alive {
        return lipgloss.NewStyle().
            Foreground(lipgloss.Color("196")).
            Render("⚠ Worker unresponsive")
    }
    
    if health.RecoveryCount > 0 {
        return lipgloss.NewStyle().
            Foreground(lipgloss.Color("214")).
            Render(fmt.Sprintf("↻ Recovered x%d", health.RecoveryCount))
    }
    
    return ""
}
```

### 6. Panic Recovery in Worker

```go
func (w *BackgroundWorker) run() {
    defer w.wg.Done()
    defer func() {
        if r := recover(); r != nil {
            log.Printf("Worker panic recovered: %v\n%s", r, debug.Stack())
            // Watchdog will detect missed heartbeats and restart
        }
    }()
    
    // ... main loop
}
```

## TESTING

```go
func TestWatchdog_DetectsDeadWorker(t *testing.T) {
    worker := createTestWorker(t)
    worker.Start()
    
    // Simulate worker death by stopping heartbeats
    worker.stopHeartbeats()
    
    // Wait for watchdog to detect
    time.Sleep(35 * time.Second)
    
    // Should have attempted recovery
    require.Greater(t, worker.recoveryCount, 0)
}

func TestWatchdog_RecoversPanickedWorker(t *testing.T) {
    worker := createTestWorker(t)
    worker.Start()
    
    // Get initial snapshot
    <-worker.snapshotCh
    
    // Inject panic on next processing
    worker.injectPanic = true
    worker.TriggerRefresh()
    
    // Wait for recovery
    time.Sleep(35 * time.Second)
    
    // Should be alive again
    health := worker.Health()
    require.True(t, health.Alive)
    require.Equal(t, 1, health.RecoveryCount)
}

func TestWatchdog_GivesUpAfterMaxRetries(t *testing.T) {
    worker := createTestWorkerWithBrokenRun(t)
    worker.Start()
    
    // Wait for all recovery attempts
    time.Sleep(2 * time.Minute)
    
    // Should have given up
    require.Equal(t, 3, worker.recoveryCount)
    
    // Should have sent unrecoverable error
    err := <-worker.errorCh
    require.False(t, err.Recoverable)
}

func TestWatchdog_HeartbeatResetAfterRecovery(t *testing.T) {
    worker := createTestWorker(t)
    worker.Start()
    
    initialHeartbeat := worker.lastHeartbeat
    time.Sleep(6 * time.Second)
    
    // Heartbeat should have updated
    require.True(t, worker.lastHeartbeat.After(initialHeartbeat))
}
```

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Worker sends heartbeat every 5 seconds
- [ ] Watchdog checks health every 10 seconds
- [ ] Recovery triggered after 30 seconds no heartbeat
- [ ] Max 3 recovery attempts before giving up
- [ ] Panic in worker caught and recovered
- [ ] Health status exposed to UI
- [ ] UI shows indicator when worker unhealthy
- [ ] Tests verify detection and recovery

## DEPENDENCIES
- Part of BackgroundWorker (bv-b94b)
